title: What do former grisly mob haunts say about modern day Greater Boston?
author: Danny McDonald, The Boston Globe
bullets: The Boston Globe The building at 12 Marshall St.
in Somerville, which was the headquarters for the Winter Hill Gang, is now the Greater Works Church of God Somerville.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in May
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Restaurants From a food truck smashburger debut in the Seaport to a celebrity-favorite steakhouse in Newton, these are the restaurants topping our must-try list this month.

May is one of the busiest months for restaurant openings and this year is no exception.

title: The Celtics are on the brink after 76ers take Game 6: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics Jayson Tatum left Thursday's game and did not return, though he says he isn't overly concerned.

Philadelphia 76ers' Paul George, left, chases a loose ball past the Boston Celtics' Neemias Queta during the second half of Game 6 in a first-round NBA basketball playoff series Thursday, April 30, 2026, in Philadelphia.
AP Photo/Matt Slocum Tyrese Maxey and the Sixers pushed the Celtics to the brink with a 106-93 victory in Game 6, sending the first-round series back to Boston for Game 7.

title: South End candy shop reports being robbed at knifepoint
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Crime “At the end of the day, we are not going to let this loser man’s actions ruin the vibe of our happy place,” the business said.
Boston Police Department A popular South End Swedish candy store was robbed at knifepoint Tuesday, police and the business said.

“At the end of the day, we are not going to let this loser man’s actions ruin the vibe of our happy place,” Madeleine’s Candy Shop, located on Clarendon Street in the South End, said in a social media post.

title: Boston Magazine names Drake Maye, Mike Vrabel as ‘Most Influential Bostonians’ for 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: New England Patriots “Yes, they lost the Super Bowl.
And it stung like the bitter cold.
But they showed this city what it looks like to overcome adversity — on the field and off.

title: Person wearing KKK robes in R.I. was pulling a social media stunt, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Local News A West Warwick resident captured video of the white-robed figure wandering around town early Monday morning.
Police in Rhode Island say they’ve confirmed that videos of a person walking around a town in a Ku Klux Klan robe and hood were part of a hoax for social media, not the work of an organized group.
Social media videos appeared to show the person walking around West Warwick while dressed in white robes and a pointed hood.

title: Report: Nearly half of immigrants arrested by ICE in Mass. had no criminal conviction or charge
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News Data shows that 45 percent of immigration arrests since 2025 involved people without criminal convictions or pending criminal cases.
FILE - Federal law enforcement officers with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and Enforcement and Removal Operations (ERO) conduct a traffic stop and detain people, Nov.
17, 2025, in Washington.

title: Boston FBI division recovers 17th century urn stolen from Italian church years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Arts The urn was reportedly stolen from the Church of San Michele Arcangelo di Cangiano sometime between 2012 and 2022.
The urn was recovered from a Northeast antiques dealer who bought it from an Italian dealer, the Boston office of the FBI said.
Boston FBI The Boston division of the FBI recovered a 17th-century reliquary urn that was stolen years ago from an Italian church, officials said.
